ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT RICH-TEX, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED AND ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY CLAIMS (ISSUES 1 AND 2) AND GRANTED AS TO THE FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIMS (ISSUES 3 AND 4). PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO THE LATE FILING OF THE MOVING PAPERS IS OVERRULED. CCP 437C PROVIDES AN OPPOSING PARTY THE RIGHT TO A MINIMUM NOTICE PERIOD OF 75 DAYS. PLAINTIFF RECEIVED SUCH NOTICE AND WAS NOT PREJUDICED BY RICH-TEX'S LATE FILING OF THE MOVING PAPERS. PLAINTIFF'S JANUARY 27, 2010 DECLARATION CREATED A TRIABLE WHETHER PLAINTIFF USED RICH-TEX ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS. RICH-TEX'S D'AMICO OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AS TO PARAGRAPH 3 OF PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION. RICH-TEX SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION REGARDING ISSUES 3 AND 4 BY PRESENTING PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY-DEVOID DISCOVERY RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER RICH-TEX MADE AN AFFIRMATIVE MISREPRESENTATION OF MATERIAL FACT TO PLAINTIFF AND WHETHER RICH-TEX ACTED WITH FRAUD, MALICE, OR OPPRESSION BY CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART, DETAILS ON RECORD. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR # 12365

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Stephen E. Carlson, Esq. of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY (415-675-7000), appearing for defendant / moving party Rich-Tex, Inc.
Katherine Allen, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party

RULING:
Defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied and its motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the negligence and strict liability claims (issues 1 and 2) and granted as to the false representation and punitive damages claims (issues 3 and 4). Plaintiff's objection to the late filing of the moving papers is overruled. CCP 437c provides an opposing party the right to a minimum notice period of 75 days. Plaintiff received such notice and was not prejudiced by Rich-Tex's late filing of the moving papers. Plaintiff's January 27, 2010 declaration created a triable whether plaintiff used Rich-Tex asbestos-containing products. Rich-Tex's D'Amico objections are overruled as to paragraph 3 of plaintiff's declaration. Rich-Tex satisfied its burden of production regarding issues 3 and 4 by presenting plaintiff's factually-devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether Rich-Tex made an affirmative misrepresentation of material fact to plaintiff and whether Rich-Tex acted with fraud, malice, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Defendant's objections were overruled in part and sustained in part, details on record. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365

James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Hudson Moore Jr.

RULING:
Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is granted. FDCC satisfied its burden of production on the issue whether Dinwiddie exposed plaintiff to any asbestos by presenting plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether the joint compound attributable to Dinwiddie to which plaintiff was exposed contained asbestos. Charles Ay's declaration lacks foundation for his opinions on this issue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR # 5208
